notes: B21 libbitcoin integration/performance evidence ledger. This is an evidence and overclaim gate, not a substitute for owner-run full-chain benchmarks.

surfaces.surface: C++ libbitcoin integration uses the direct secp256k1::fastsecp256k1_libbitcoin target
surfaces.claim_scope: Integration hygiene: libbitcoin call sites link the direct C++ target and do not require ufsecp C ABI, shim, or bridge symbols.
surfaces.reproduce_command: cmake -S . -B out/libbitcoin-test -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN=ON -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN_TESTS=ON && cmake --build out/libbitcoin-test -j && ctest --test-dir out/libbitcoin-test -R lbtc_direct --output-on-failure

surfaces.surface: direct C++ libbitcoin batch benchmark emits a structured JSON artifact
surfaces.claim_scope: Benchmark evidence format: local throughput numbers must be recorded with target_context, claim_scope, batch, iters, pool, C ABI/shim/bridge requirement flags, and timing.
surfaces.reproduce_command: cmake -S . -B out/libbitcoin-bench -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN=ON -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN_BENCH=ON && cmake --build out/libbitcoin-bench --target bench_lbtc_direct_batch -j && out/libbitcoin-bench/compat/libbitcoin_direct/bench_lbtc_direct_batch 1000000 5 50000 --json out/libbitcoin-bench/lbtc_direct_batch.json

surfaces.surface: CUDA libbitcoin opaque ECDSA row path reuses grow-only device buffers across batches
surfaces.claim_scope: Implementation optimization: removes per-batch cudaMalloc/cudaFree from the consensus-bearing libbitcoin row path while preserving direct opaque-row parsing on device.

surfaces.surface: MSVC Windows libbitcoin profile remains a first-class measured target
surfaces.claim_scope: Owner-measured Windows/MSVC profile. A per-hardware JSON bench artifact is now attached (Intel i7-11700, 8C/16T, MSVC 19.44.35227, dev 07a1eceee): the _mt batch-verify path scales 8.11x (ECDSA) / 5.24x (Schnorr) at 16 threads vs the t=1 serial baseline. This is a relative scaling artifact for the MSVC profile on this machine, NOT a cross-hardware absolute-throughput claim; absolute per-core throughput stays MSVC-bound (clang-cl is the faster Windows codegen).
surfaces.reproduce_command: cmake -S . -B out/libbitcoin-msvc -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN=ON -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN_BENCH=ON && cmake --build out/libbitcoin-msvc --target bench_lbtc_direct_batch -j && out\\libbitcoin-msvc\\compat\\libbitcoin_direct\\bench_lbtc_direct_batch.exe 1000000 5 50000 --json out\\libbitcoin-msvc\\lbtc_direct_msvc.json

surfaces.surface: Vertical/column API accepts libbitcoin opaque ECDSA signatures while documenting the current scratch-normalization boundary
surfaces.claim_scope: Interface contract: columns keep the opaque signature format by default; row API is current no-intermediate-table GPU path, while columns remain explicitly tracked so no native no-copy GPU-column claim can be made accidentally.

surfaces.surface: CAAS gate validates libbitcoin performance/integration evidence before claims are trusted
surfaces.claim_scope: Audit discipline: libbitcoin performance evidence must name target_context, claim_scope, reproduce command, evidence paths, and copy/native-hardware policy.

surfaces.surface: Benchmark JSON content gate for libbitcoin-shaped GPU workload evidence
surfaces.claim_scope: Closes the confirmed gap that lbtc_caas_perf_matrix_gate only checks evidence_paths file existence, never JSON content: rejects zero/negative timing, arithmetic-inconsistent throughput, missing/mistyped fields, backend/device/driver_version contradictions, GPU claims without provider_linked+hook_installed, CPU-only rows relabeled gpu_acceleration, non-matched_reference validation, and one-sided speedup_vs_cpu_forced claims, for both the current bench_lbtc_public_ops artifact schema and the future phase-aware libbitcoin GPU workload schema.
surfaces.reproduce_command: cmake --build out/libbitcoin-bench -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN=ON -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN_BENCH=ON && cmake --build out/libbitcoin-bench --target bench_lbtc_public_ops -j && out/libbitcoin-bench/compat/libbitcoin_direct/bench_lbtc_public_ops 128 1 80 128 64 128 --json /tmp/lbtc_public_ops_evidence_smoke.json && python3 ci/check_lbtc_gpu_workload_evidence.py /tmp/lbtc_public_ops_evidence_smoke.json && python3 ci/test_lbtc_gpu_workload_evidence.py

surfaces.surface: Direct C++ GPU column verify — hook-installation runtime smoke test in the direct profile
surfaces.claim_scope: Transparent GPU column acceleration: the GpuColumnsVerifyHook must be non-null after GPU-capable startup in the libbitcoin direct profile (not only in the C ABI audit test). When the companion backend card lbtc-direct-gpu-backend-columns adds a runtime hook smoke target to the direct test suite, this entry moves to accepted.

surfaces.surface: libbitcoin-shaped workload benchmark harness (txid_batch, wtxid_batch, merkle_pair_batch, merkle_root_batch) emitting schema-v2 evidence artifacts
surfaces.claim_scope: Every workload always emits a backend=cpu/evidence_class=api_correctness/mode=direct-cpu-forced row, validated against an independent (non-library) oracle. On a host where a real GPU backend is linked, initialized, ready, and the direct workload hook accepts the exact benchmark shape (identified via GpuTelemetry, see lbtc_gpu_workload_backend_telemetry below), that workload ALSO emits a paired backend!=cpu/evidence_class=gpu_acceleration/mode=direct-production row, validated against the SAME independent oracle, with a real (non-fabricated) speedup_vs_cpu_forced ratio. On a CPU-only host, or when the direct hook declines the benchmark shape, only the api_correctness row is emitted -- absence of handled GPU evidence is reported honestly, never papered over. sighash_batch is intentionally excluded (descriptor contract not accepted, see workingdocs/libbitcoin_gpu_workloads/api_plan_blocker_resolution_deepseek.md blocker B1). Reviewer smoke on 2026-07-09 with the OpenCL provider linked showed all 4 workload hooks declining the benchmark shapes; the resulting CPU-only artifacts all passed ci/check_lbtc_gpu_workload_evidence.py. DIAGNOSED same day (workingdocs/libbitcoin_gpu_workloads/hook_decline_diagnostics_claude.json): root cause was CWD-relative kernel-file resolution in OpenCLBackend::ensure_extended_kernels() (src/gpu/src/gpu_backend_opencl.cpp), not a defect in any of the 4 workloads, their hooks, or the merkle_pair/merkle_root backend kernels -- confirmed by a standalone one-shot probe (secp256k1_extended.cl not found from a typical invocation CWD) and by re-running with the source file manually staged next to the bench binary, which let every op (including merkle_pair_hash) reach and pass the real OpenCL/RTX-5060-Ti device. FOLLOW-UP same day (task lbtc-direct-bench-kernel-staging-claude; report at parent-repo-root workingdocs/libbitcoin_gpu_workloads/direct_bench_kernel_staging_claude.json, a taskctl coordination artifact, not a submodule-relative evidence path): compat/libbitcoin_direct/CMakeLists.txt now stages secp256k1_extended.cl + its 6 transitive includes into kernels/ next to bench_lbtc_workloads/bench_lbtc_public_ops (POST_BUILD, gated on an OpenCL-backed libbitcoin GPU bench). Reproducing with `cmake -E chdir` into that directory, all 4 workloads (txid_batch, wtxid_batch, merkle_pair_batch, merkle_root_batch) reached backend=opencl/device=NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 Ti/evidence_class=gpu_acceleration with a real speedup_vs_cpu_forced ratio, validated against each workload's independent oracle. The backend resolver fix landed separately in commit a9e0c25d and independently finds the kernel via executable-relative walk-up for in-tree build directories with no staging; the kernels/ copy here is a build-layout-independent fallback for out-of-tree/package layouts. gpu_backend_opencl.cpp itself was not edited by this task.
surfaces.reproduce_command: cmake -S . -B out/libbitcoin-bench -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN=ON -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN_BENCH=ON -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN_GPU=ON -DSECP256K1_BUILD_OPENCL=ON && cmake --build out/libbitcoin-bench --target bench_lbtc_workloads -j && mkdir -p /tmp/lbtc_workloads_smoke && cmake -E chdir out/libbitcoin-bench/compat/libbitcoin_direct ./bench_lbtc_workloads --batch-class small --iters 5 --json-dir /tmp/lbtc_workloads_smoke && for f in /tmp/lbtc_workloads_smoke/*.json; do python3 ci/check_lbtc_gpu_workload_evidence.py "$f"; done

surfaces.surface: GpuTelemetry hook -- lets libbitcoin-direct benchmark harnesses honestly identify the linked GPU backend name/id/device without routing through the C ABI or bridge
surfaces.claim_scope: Benchmark/evidence-only telemetry extension of the existing self-installing lbtc GPU hook layer. Populated strictly from the already-existing secp256k1::gpu::GpuBackend::backend_id()/backend_name()/device_info() virtuals (gpu_backend.hpp) -- adds no new backend method and required zero edits to gpu_backend.hpp or any *_cuda.cu/*_opencl.cpp/*_metal.mm backend file. driver_version is NOT sourced by this telemetry (DeviceInfo carries no driver field); benchmark artifacts report driver_version=null on GPU rows as an honest gap, never a fabricated string. Telemetry is only used to label a row after the benchmark independently proves the direct hook accepted and matched the batch; otherwise rows remain backend=cpu/device=n/a. Never called from any production/hot-path code -- only bench_workloads.cpp and bench_public_ops.cpp opt in. Sibling extension added 2026-07-09: g_lbtc_gpu_last_error_hook / GpuLastError, sourced strictly from the already-existing GpuBackend::last_error()/last_error_msg() virtuals through the SAME cached backend probe, prints a bounded stderr-only decline reason next to every 'GPU hook declined'/'did not independently handle the batch' message -- never written into any JSON artifact, never changes backend/evidence_class.
surfaces.reproduce_command: cmake -S . -B out/libbitcoin-bench -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN=ON -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN_BENCH=ON -DSECP256K1_BUILD_LIBBITCOIN_GPU=ON -DSECP256K1_BUILD_OPENCL=ON && cmake --build out/libbitcoin-bench --target bench_lbtc_public_ops -j && out/libbitcoin-bench/compat/libbitcoin_direct/bench_lbtc_public_ops 128 1 80 128 64 128 --json /tmp/lbtc_public_ops_telemetry_smoke.json && python3 -c "import json;d=json.load(open('/tmp/lbtc_public_ops_telemetry_smoke.json'));print([r['backend'] for r in d['results'] if r['mode']=='direct-production'])"
